items.title: Trump cancels Kushner, Witkoff trip to Pakistan for Iran talks

items.content: U.S. envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were expected to head to Islamabad Saturday, but President Trump said later that his "representatives" would not be going.

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Donald Trump cancels US envoy trip to Pakistan for ceasefire talks with Iran
items.standfirst: US president says ‘too much time’ has been ‘wasted on traveling’ as Israel strikes Lebanon, claiming Hezbollah also launched rocketsWe have some images coming through the newswires of Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, speaking with Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, and other officials in Islamabad this morning.Araghchi arrived in Pakistan last night. He wrote on social media that his trip would focus on “bilateral matters and regional developments”. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says ‘too much time’ has been ‘wasted on traveling’ as Israel strikes Lebanon, claiming Hezbollah also launched rocketsWe have some images coming through the newswires of Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, speaking with Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, and other officials in Islamabad this morning.Araghchi arrived in Pakistan last night. He wrote on social media that his trip would focus on “bilateral matters and regional developments”. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: U.S. envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are set to head to Islamabad for more Iran peace talks, but it's still unclear if Iran will participate in direct talks with the U.S.
items.content: U.S. envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are set to head to Islamabad for more Iran peace talks, but it's still unclear if Iran will participate in direct talks with the U.S.

items.title: Eye Opener: Severe storms, tornadoes slam Midwest
items.standfirst: Severe storms and tornadoes hit the Midwest, leaving thousands without power. Meanwhile, U.S. officials are set to head to Pakistan for more Iran talks, but it remains unclear if Iran will participate in direct talks with the U.S. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.
items.content: Severe storms and tornadoes hit the Midwest, leaving thousands without power. Meanwhile, U.S. officials are set to head to Pakistan for more Iran talks, but it remains unclear if Iran will participate in direct talks with the U.S. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

items.title: US envoys to travel to Islamabad despite Iran ruling out direct negotiations with US
items.standfirst: US envoys are expected to travel to Pakistan on Saturday in a new bid to salvage ceasefire talks with Tehran, even as Iran ruled out direct negotiations with US representatives as its top diplomat arrived in Islamabad. The latest effort to broker a deal comes as an indefinite ceasefire has paused most fighting, but the economic fallout is still mounting with global energy shipments disrupted by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: US envoys are expected to travel to Pakistan on Saturday in a new bid to salvage ceasefire talks with Tehran, even as Iran ruled out direct negotiations with US representatives as its top diplomat arrived in Islamabad. The latest effort to broker a deal comes as an indefinite ceasefire has paused most fighting, but the economic fallout is still mounting with global energy shipments disrupted by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
items.standfirst: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks
items.content: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)

items.title: US envoy and Trump’s son-in-law to travel to Pakistan amid hopes for renewed Iran peace talks – as it happened
items.standfirst: This live blog is now closed. For the latest, read our full report:Trump sending Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan to resume talks on Iran warThe EU’s foreign chief has said that talks with Iran should include nuclear experts otherwise “we will end up with a more dangerous Iran.”Speaking on Friday ahead of an informal summit of EU leaders in Cyprus, EU’s foreign chief Kaja Kallas said: “If the talks are only about the nuclear and there are no nuclear experts around the table, then we will end up with an agreement that is weaker than the JCPOA was.” Continue reading...
items.content: This live blog is now closed. For the latest, read our full report:Trump sending Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan to resume talks on Iran warThe EU’s foreign chief has said that talks with Iran should include nuclear experts otherwise “we will end up with a more dangerous Iran.”Speaking on Friday ahead of an informal summit of EU leaders in Cyprus, EU’s foreign chief Kaja Kallas said: “If the talks are only about the nuclear and there are no nuclear experts around the table, then we will end up with an agreement that is weaker than the JCPOA was.” Continue reading...

items.title: Trump sending Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan to resume talks on Iran war
items.standfirst: White House says its Middle East envoys will meet Tehran’s foreign minister in Islamabad Middle East crisis – live updatesDonald Trump is sending his Middle East envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan to resume negotiations to end the war with Iran, which has lasted nearly eight weeks.The White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt confirmed the travel on Friday, saying that Witkoff and Kushner, Trump’s son-in-law, would meet Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, in Islamabad. Continue reading...
items.content: White House says its Middle East envoys will meet Tehran’s foreign minister in Islamabad Middle East crisis – live updatesDonald Trump is sending his Middle East envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan to resume negotiations to end the war with Iran, which has lasted nearly eight weeks.The White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt confirmed the travel on Friday, saying that Witkoff and Kushner, Trump’s son-in-law, would meet Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, in Islamabad. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch co-ordinated attacks across Mali
clusters.neutral_headline: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch co-ordinated attacks across Mali
bullet_summary: BAMAKO, Mali — Gunmen attacked several locations in Mali's capital and other cities early Saturday in a possible coordinated assault, residents and authorities said
The army in junta-ruled Mali said on Saturday it was battling what it called “terrorist groups” that launched surprise attacks around the capital Bamako and other parts of the West African nation
FRANCE 24 journalist Justice Baidoo reported that the FLA has largely been confined to northern Mali in recent years
Clashes between the Malian army and rebel groups are being reported in cities across the country

items.title: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch co-ordinated attacks across Mali
items.standfirst: Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north, in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.
items.content: Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north, in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.

items.title: Mali hit by wave of coordinated attacks from armed groups
items.standfirst: Gunfire and explosions have rocked Mali's capital Bamako and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups, including jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.
items.content: Gunfire and explosions have rocked Mali's capital Bamako and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups, including jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.(Image credit: uncredited)

items.title: Gunfire and blasts rock Mali as attackers hit capital and other cities, residents say
items.standfirst: BAMAKO, Mali — Gunmen attacked several locations in Mali's capital and other cities early Saturday in a possible coordinated assault, residents and authorities said
items.content: BAMAKO, Mali — Gunmen attacked several locations in Mali's capital and other cities early Saturday in a possible coordinated assault, residents and authorities said

items.title: FLA claims attacks on Malian capital
items.standfirst: The army in junta-ruled Mali said on Saturday it was battling what it called “terrorist groups” that launched surprise attacks around the capital Bamako and other parts of the West African nation. The attacks were claimed by the Azawad Liberation Front, a separatist group that has been fighting the Malian army for more than a decade. FRANCE 24 journalist Justice Baidoo reported that the FLA has largely been confined to northern Mali in recent years.
items.content: The army in junta-ruled Mali said on Saturday it was battling what it called “terrorist groups” that launched surprise attacks around the capital Bamako and other parts of the West African nation. The attacks were claimed by the Azawad Liberation Front, a separatist group that has been fighting the Malian army for more than a decade. FRANCE 24 journalist Justice Baidoo reported that the FLA has largely been confined to northern Mali in recent years.

items.title: ‘Scale and coordination of Mali attacks appear unprecedented’
items.standfirst: Clashes between the Malian army and rebel groups are being reported in cities across the country.
items.content: Clashes between the Malian army and rebel groups are being reported in cities across the country.

items.title: Armed groups stage simultaneous attacks across Mali
items.standfirst: Armed groups have attacked Mali’s capital, its main airport, and several locations across the country.
items.content: Armed groups have attacked Mali’s capital, its main airport, and several locations across the country.

items.title: Coordinated attacks hit Bamako and Northern Mali
items.standfirst: Gunmen launched a wave of coordinated assaults across Mali early Saturday, striking the capital Bamako as well as key northern cities including Kidal and Gao. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo reports.
items.content: Gunmen launched a wave of coordinated assaults across Mali early Saturday, striking the capital Bamako as well as key northern cities including Kidal and Gao. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo reports.

items.title: Gunfire rocks Bamako: 'Too soon to tell' if junta will fall
items.standfirst: Mali's army said Saturday that it was fighting "terrorist groups" that had attacked army barracks in the capital Bamako and other areas in the African nation. Witnesses reported gunfire in several cities in the military-ruled state and an army statement said: "Terrorist groups, not yet identified, early this morning targeted certain points and barracks in the capital and the interior." FRANCE 24's Wassim Nasr said it is still "too soon to tell" if the country's military junta will fall.
items.content: Mali's army said Saturday that it was fighting "terrorist groups" that had attacked army barracks in the capital Bamako and other areas in the African nation. Witnesses reported gunfire in several cities in the military-ruled state and an army statement said: "Terrorist groups, not yet identified, early this morning targeted certain points and barracks in the capital and the interior." FRANCE 24's Wassim Nasr said it is still "too soon to tell" if the country's military junta will fall.

items.title: Terrorists launch simultaneous attacks in Mali, authorities say
items.standfirst: Terrorists have carried out attacks in various locations across Mali, according to the country's army.
items.content: Terrorists have carried out attacks in various locations across Mali, according to the country's army.

items.title: Mali attacks updates: Coordinated attacks in Bamako, Kidal and elsewhere
items.standfirst: These were the updates about the attacks across Mali on Saturday, April 25, 2026.
items.content: These were the updates about the attacks across Mali on Saturday, April 25, 2026.

items.title: Mali live : Army clashes with ‘terrorist’ groups, Kidal captured, Tuareg rebels say
items.standfirst: Gunmen attacked several locations in Mali's capital and other cities early Saturday. Mali’s army said its soldiers were “engaged in eliminating the attackers”. Meanwhile, Tuareg rebels of the Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) coalition said they had captured the northern city of Kidal. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Gunmen attacked several locations in Mali's capital and other cities early Saturday. Mali’s army said its soldiers were “engaged in eliminating the attackers”. Meanwhile, Tuareg rebels of the Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) coalition said they had captured the northern city of Kidal. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Gunmen stage simultaneous attacks across Mali, army says
items.standfirst: Capital Bamako and several other locations hit in an apparently coordinated ⁠assault involving multiple groups.
items.content: Capital Bamako and several other locations hit in an apparently coordinated ⁠assault involving multiple groups.

clusters.title: Russian strikes kill several in Ukraine's Dnipro
clusters.neutral_headline: Russian airstrikes kill at least seven people in Ukraine overnight
bullet_summary: Russian strikes across Ukraine killed at least six people, officials said Saturday, most of them in an attack on a residential building in the eastern city of Dnipro
The bodies of four people were found in the ruins of a house destroyed in the attacks, and workers continued to search for bodies on Saturday morning

items.title: Russian strikes kill several in Ukraine's Dnipro
items.standfirst: Russian strikes across Ukraine killed at least six people, officials said Saturday, most of them in an attack on a residential building in the eastern city of Dnipro.
items.content: Russian strikes across Ukraine killed at least six people, officials said Saturday, most of them in an attack on a residential building in the eastern city of Dnipro.

items.title: Russian airstrikes kill at least seven people in Ukraine overnight
items.standfirst: Dnipro bore the brunt of the attacks but Odesa and Kharkiv were also targeted in largest onslaught for several daysRussian drone and missile strikes across Ukraine killed at least seven people overnight, including five in the city of Dnipro, Ukrainian local authorities have said.Reports say that at least 34 people have been injured in the strikes, which lasted “practically all night”, according to the Dnipropetrovsk regional head, Oleksandr Hanzha. The bodies of four people were found in the ruins of a house destroyed in the attacks, and workers continued to search for bodies on Saturday morning. Continue reading...
items.content: Dnipro bore the brunt of the attacks but Odesa and Kharkiv were also targeted in largest onslaught for several daysRussian drone and missile strikes across Ukraine killed at least seven people overnight, including five in the city of Dnipro, Ukrainian local authorities have said.Reports say that at least 34 people have been injured in the strikes, which lasted “practically all night”, according to the Dnipropetrovsk regional head, Oleksandr Hanzha. The bodies of four people were found in the ruins of a house destroyed in the attacks, and workers continued to search for bodies on Saturday morning. Continue reading...

items.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
items.standfirst: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.
items.content: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

items.title: Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: The Justice Department ended its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's renovation project, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro announced Friday.
items.content: The Justice Department ended its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's renovation project, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro announced Friday.

items.title: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.
items.content: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

items.title: US justice department drops criminal investigation against Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: Move will remove obstacle for confirmation of Kevin Warsh, Trump’s pick to replace Powell as Federal Reserve chairSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US Department of Justice is dropping its criminal investigation against the Federal Reserve chair, Jerome Powell, clearing the path for Donald Trump’s new nominee for chair to be confirmed.Jeanine Pirro, Trump’s appointed US attorney for the District of Columbia, said in a social media post that she had directed her office to close its investigation into renovations at the Fed headquarters that went over budget. Continue reading...

items.title: Pope Leo reiterates opposition to death penalty on same day U.S. approves firing squads
items.standfirst: Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.
items.content: Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.(Image credit: Christopher Furlong)

items.title: Justice Department to allow firing squads for executions in move to ramp up capital punishment
items.standfirst: The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.
items.content: The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)

items.content: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.

clusters.title: Low voter turnout in Gaza as West Bank holds local elections
clusters.neutral_headline: Palestinians cast their ballots as Gaza holds first election in 21 years
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Al Jazeera

items.title: Low voter turnout in Gaza as West Bank holds local elections
items.standfirst: Palestinians in the West Bank and central Gaza voted Saturday in municipal elections, the first since the war in Gaza erupted, with low turnout and a limited political field. Nearly 1.5 million people are registered to vote in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, as well as 70,000 people in Gaza's Deir el-Balah area, according to the Ramallah-based Central Elections Commission.
items.content: Palestinians in the West Bank and central Gaza voted Saturday in municipal elections, the first since the war in Gaza erupted, with low turnout and a limited political field. Nearly 1.5 million people are registered to vote in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, as well as 70,000 people in Gaza's Deir el-Balah area, according to the Ramallah-based Central Elections Commission.

items.title: Palestinians cast their ballots as Gaza holds first election in 21 years

clusters.title: Spain PM brushes of reported Pentagon email on punishing NATO members
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain PM brushes of reported Pentagon email on punishing NATO members
bullet_summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war
Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: Spain PM brushes of reported Pentagon email on punishing NATO members
items.standfirst: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Friday refused to be drawn into a dispute with the United States over reports that the Pentagon is weighing whether to punish members of NATO that fail to support American operations in the Iran war. Among those in the firing line is Spain, which has refused to allow US forces involved in the war to use bases on its territory or airspace. Spain says that US-Israeli actions in the Iran war contravenes international law.
items.content: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Friday refused to be drawn into a dispute with the United States over reports that the Pentagon is weighing whether to punish members of NATO that fail to support American operations in the Iran war. Among those in the firing line is Spain, which has refused to allow US forces involved in the war to use bases on its territory or airspace. Spain says that US-Israeli actions in the Iran war contravenes international law.

items.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
items.standfirst: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.
items.content: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.
